name: Rust SPA Frameworks
description: Frameworks and libraries for building Single Page Applications and full-stack web apps in Rust, compared across reactivity model, WASM toolchain, rendering and full-stack story, routing, styling, and ecosystem

groups.name: General Information
groups.description: Basic identifying information, licensing, and maintenance status

attributes.name: Description
attributes.description: One-line value proposition of the framework

attributes.name: License
attributes.description: Primary open-source license(s); note dual-licensing in a comment

attributes.name: Repository
attributes.description: Primary source repository (GitHub, GitLab)

attributes.name: Crates.io
attributes.description: Top-level framework crate page on crates.io

attributes.name: Documentation
attributes.description: Primary documentation site (book, docs.rs, or custom guide)

attributes.name: First Release
attributes.description: Year of first public release or first crates.io publish

attributes.name: Latest Stable Version
attributes.description: Current stable version from crates.io

attributes.name: Latest Release
attributes.description: Date of the most recent crates.io release

attributes.name: Maintenance Status
attributes.description: Based on commits/releases in the last 6 months

attributes.name: Maturity
attributes.description: Based on API stability, production usage, and 1.0 status

attributes.name: Corporate/Foundation Backing
attributes.description: Company or org funding development, or "Community" if none

groups.name: Architecture & Reactivity
groups.description: Reactivity model, component and view authoring, and state management

attributes.name: Reactivity Model
attributes.description: Primary mechanism by which state changes propagate; tag all that apply

attributes.name: Component Model
attributes.description: How components are defined

attributes.name: View Authoring
attributes.description: How views are written; tag all supported

attributes.name: Fine-Grained Updates
attributes.description: Updates target the exact DOM nodes affected (no full-subtree re-render/diff)

attributes.name: Uses Virtual DOM
attributes.description: Maintains a virtual DOM and diffs it against the previous render

attributes.name: Async/Await in Components
attributes.description: Components/resources can natively .await async data loading

attributes.name: Global State Solution
attributes.description: How app-wide shared state is handled

groups.name: WASM & Build Toolchain
groups.description: Compilation target, build tooling, bundle size, and JS interop

attributes.name: Compiles To
attributes.description: Compilation target

attributes.name: Primary Build Tool
attributes.description: Officially recommended build toolchain

attributes.name: Official CLI
attributes.description: First-party scaffolding/build/dev-server CLI

attributes.name: Dev Server / HMR
attributes.description: Dev-server reload capability

attributes.name: Minimal WASM Bundle Size
attributes.description: Release build of a minimal hello-world app, .wasm gzipped; smaller is better. Record version/profile/date in a comment

attributes.name: JS Interop
attributes.description: Mechanisms exposed for talking to browser/JS APIs

attributes.name: JS Interop Ergonomics
attributes.description: 5 = calling JS / using web APIs is smooth and well-documented; 1 = awkward, poorly documented

attributes.name: no_std / Minimal Runtime
attributes.description: Can build without full std or with a deliberately minimal runtime footprint

groups.name: Rendering & Full-Stack
groups.description: Client and server rendering, hydration, and full-stack server-function story

attributes.name: Client-Side Rendering (SPA)
attributes.description: Supports a pure client-rendered SPA mode (baseline requirement for inclusion)

attributes.name: Server-Side Rendering
attributes.description: Can render HTML on the server for first paint

attributes.name: Static Site Generation
attributes.description: Can pre-render routes to static HTML at build time

attributes.name: Hydration
attributes.description: Hydration modes supported

attributes.name: Server Functions
attributes.description: Integrated, isomorphic server-function mechanism (call server logic from client code, wiring generated)

attributes.name: Streaming SSR
attributes.description: Supports streaming server-rendered HTML

attributes.name: Backend Integration
attributes.description: Server frameworks it integrates with for full-stack mode

attributes.name: Isomorphic Routing
attributes.description: The same router resolves routes identically on server and client

attributes.name: Official Router
attributes.description: First-party routing solution shipped or maintained by the core team

attributes.name: Router Name
attributes.description: Name of the router crate/module, or null

attributes.name: Nested Routes
attributes.description: Supports nested/child route layouts

attributes.name: Dynamic Route Params
attributes.description: Parameterized routes (e.g. /user/:id)

attributes.name: Typed Routes
attributes.description: Routes are type-checked (enum/derive-based) rather than string-matched

attributes.name: Route Guards
attributes.description: Navigation guards / redirect hooks before entering a route

attributes.name: Lazy / Code-Split Routes
attributes.description: Routes can be code-split and loaded on demand

groups.name: UI & Styling
groups.description: Styling approaches, component ecosystem, forms, and UI primitives

attributes.name: Styling Approach
attributes.description: First-class styling approaches

attributes.name: Scoped Styles
attributes.description: Built-in component-scoped styling (styles don't leak globally)

attributes.name: Component Library Ecosystem
attributes.description: 5 = several mature UI component libraries; 3 = a few community kits; 1 = essentially none

attributes.name: Forms & Inputs
attributes.description: How form state is handled

attributes.name: SVG / Canvas Support
attributes.description: First-class SVG authoring in the view macro / interop for canvas

attributes.name: Suspense / Loading UI
attributes.description: Declarative loading boundaries for async resources

attributes.name: Error Boundaries
attributes.description: Catch and render fallback UI for errors in the component tree

groups.name: Developer Experience
groups.description: Documentation, ergonomics, tooling, and testing support

attributes.name: Documentation Quality
attributes.description: 5 = book + tutorials + API docs + examples; 3 = adequate API docs with some examples; 1 = minimal/outdated

attributes.name: API Ergonomics
attributes.description: 5 = idiomatic Rust, minimal boilerplate, good error messages; 3 = functional but verbose; 1 = heavy boilerplate or lifetime friction

attributes.name: Compile Times
attributes.description: Relative to peer Rust web frameworks for a small app

attributes.name: Examples Count
attributes.description: Official repo examples: extensive >20, good 10-20, limited 3-10, minimal <3

attributes.name: Interactive Playground
attributes.description: URL to an online playground/REPL/example gallery, or null

attributes.name: Browser DevTools
attributes.description: Dedicated devtools/inspector (component tree, signals) beyond generic browser tools

attributes.name: Testing Support
attributes.description: Test story the framework documents/supports

groups.name: Ecosystem & Community
groups.description: Adoption metrics, community health, and surrounding ecosystem

attributes.name: GitHub Stars
attributes.description: Snapshot star count; note the date

attributes.name: Crates.io Downloads
attributes.description: All-time downloads of the top-level crate

attributes.name: Contributors
attributes.description: Approximate GitHub contributor count

attributes.name: Community Size
attributes.description: Rough size of the active community

attributes.name: Ecosystem Crates
attributes.description: Availability of routers, component kits, and integrations

attributes.name: Notable Production Users
attributes.description: Shipping apps/companies known to use it; null if none widely documented

attributes.name: Meta-Framework / Full-Stack Kit
attributes.description: Name of the associated full-stack layer, or null
